text: The surgeon identifies an ischemic zone on the specimen side and proceeds to make an enterotomy approximately three to four centimeters in size. The anvil, previously placed into the abdomen through the right lower quadrant port site, is prepared for insertion. A gel port is used to seal the location, facilitating both extraction and robotic instrumentation.

The surgeon inserts the anvil into the collotomy, advancing it proximally along the bowel. The V-lock suture is used to secure the anvil's position and guide the spike's exit point, ensuring the green anvil portion remains away from the staple line.

The surgeon pulls the anvil out using the V-lock suture, then uses the same V-lock to close the colotomy on the specimen side. The green anvil spike is removed by inserting the suture needle from the V-lock and performing a quick pop-off maneuver.
